concerns.text: FR-001 requires downloading/parsing ACE/WIND composition data. The plan (research.md) explicitly states 'No verified source for WIND spacecraft composition exists' and 'SWICS: NO verified source found'. The plan pivots to 'Synthetic Data Generation' instead of implementing the ingestion logic for real data. This leaves FR-001 unbacked by a real-data implementation task.

FR-002 requires downloading/parsing NOAA Dst/Kp indices. The plan (research.md) states 'No verified source for Dst/Kp indices exists in the provided list' and pivots to synthetic data. The plan lacks a task to implement the ingestion of real NOAA indices, leaving FR-002 unbacked.

FR-003 requires aligning time series to 1-hour resolution. While the plan mentions alignment logic, it is predicated on the synthetic data generation step. There is no explicit task or phase dedicated to the alignment of *real* heterogeneous time-series data (64s vs 1-hour) as required by the spec's acceptance scenarios.

FR-008 mandates a block permutation test on composition coefficients. The plan's 'Synthetic Data' approach validates the *pipeline* but does not validate the statistical significance of *real* composition ratios (since they are simulated). The spec requires testing the hypothesis on real data; the plan's pivot to synthetic data leaves the core statistical validation of the hypothesis unbacked.

SC-001 requires rejecting the null hypothesis based on permutation-derived p-values from real data. The plan explicitly states the study will use synthetic data to 'validate the statistical pipeline' and 'report this as a data limitation'. This means the success criterion (measuring real ΔR² and p-values) cannot be met by the current plan.
